## figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map, list_code_components, get_code_component_info)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (load first when available):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_figma flows
- /figma-generate-diagram — MANDATORY before calling generate_diagram

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branc… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. Routes to type-specific guidance (generic flowchart, architecture flowchart) and tells you when to proceed directly, when to use a different diagram type, or when the tool isn't the right fit at all.
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-design-system-rules: Generates custom design system rules for the user's codebase. Use when user says "create design system rules", "generate rules for my project", "set up design rules", "customize design system guidelines", or wants to establish project-specific conventions for Figma-to-code workflows. Requires Figma MCP server connection.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-implement-design: Translates Figma designs into production-ready application code with 1:1 visual fidelity. Use when implementing UI code from Figma files, when user mentions "implement design", "generate code", "implement component", provides Figma URLs, or asks to build components matching Figma specs. For Figma canvas writes via `use_figma`, use `figma-use`.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
392	## `interest_core_lib` — user-interest catalog / matchmaking enrichment
393	
394	**What it does.** Wraps external catalog APIs (TMDB movie+TV, Spotify, Google Books, Wikiart) plus an internal Solr-backed core-values/activities catalog behind one uniform "interest item" shape. Used to enrich a user's declared interests with structured, matchmaking-relevant attributes (genres, cast/creators, audio features, etc.).
395	
396	**Key modules.**
397	- `interest_core_lib/interest_core_lib.py` — composition root; registers handlers from Hydra config, owns the Solr client + cache.
398	- `interest_core_lib/data_layers/service/interest_service.py` — `InterestService.search/get/suggestions` with `@Cache` + `handle_reconnect` (one retry that reconnects the handler, then maps to `StatusCodeException`).
399	- `interest_core_lib/client/interest_handler.py` — `InterestHandler` ABC. Concrete handlers under `client/<domain>/`; test doubles under `mocks/`.
400	
401	**The "interest item" contract (hidden, test-enforced).** Every `InterestHandler.to_interest_item(...)` must return a dict with the common base keys (`external_id`, `label`, `description`, `image_url`, `thumbnail_url`, `preview_url`, `url`, `source`, `creator`, `meta_data`) plus domain-specific matchmaking fields. There is **no shared schema class** — the key contract lives in each domain's `tests/<domain>_test.py` `keys_list`, asserted via `assertIn(key, item)` against **live API calls** (real config keys required; not run in offline CI). When you add a field to a handler's `to_interest_item`, add it to that domain's `keys_list` too. `books_test.py` is the exception — it only prints, no `keys_list`. `tests/interest_data_test.py` complements those: it is **offline and deterministic** (fakes the network/SDK calls) and asserts the extracted *values* — including the movie-vs-TV / search-vs-info dual-path fallbacks and the graceful-degradation failure paths — so it runs in CI without credentials and is the place to add value-level assertions for new fields.
402	
403	**Dual call path — `to_interest_item` runs on two differently-shaped inputs.** `InterestHandler.search()` calls `to_interest_item` on each *search-result* element; `InterestHandler.info()` calls `get_full_info()` then `to_interest_item` on the *full-info* object. Handlers must read fields defensively because the same method sees both shapes (e.g. TMDB movie vs TV: `release_date`/`original_title` vs `first_air_date`/`original_name`; TMDB `_to_interest_search_result` always re-fetches via `_get_full_info` so cast/genres/keywords work on both paths). Extra per-item enrichment calls (TMDB keywords, Spotify artist genres) are wrapped in try/except + `logger.warning` so one failing sub-call never drops the whole item.
404	
405	**Depends on / consumed by.** Depends on `core-lib` (Service/Cache/Solr/ClientBase) and external APIs via `tmdbsimple`/`spotipy`/HTTP. Standalone `*-core-lib`; follows the framework conventions below (Hydra config at `config/interest_core_lib.yaml`, onion layers, singleton-style main class). No owned SQL tables — its catalog store is Solr.
406	
407	---
408	
409	# core-lib framework conventions
410	
411	Every `*-core-lib` package in this workspace (present or future) follows the layout and idioms below. This section is stable — the details come straight from `core-lib/docs/pages/core_lib_doc/`.
412	
413	## Philosophy
414	
415	- **Onion Architecture.** Three concentric layers: `data → data_access → service`. Outer layers may call inner; never the reverse. Business logic and caching belong in `service`, DB access in `data_access`, entities/connectors in `data`.
416	- **POPO** (Plain Old Python Object). No framework lock-in. A core-lib should run headless in tests and be pluggable into Flask, Django, or anything else.
417	- **Composable.** A core-lib is a plugin. Embed another via `instantiate_config(self.config, OtherCoreLib)` from `core_lib.helpers.config_instances`.
418	- **Loose coupling via Hydra.** All wiring is YAML-driven; config is passed in as `DictConfig`.
419	
420	## Canonical folder tree
421	
422	```
423	<name>-core-lib/                         # project directory (the git repo)
424	├── <name>_core_lib/                     # main code package (underscored)
425	│   ├── config/
426	│   │   └── <name>_core_lib.yaml         # Hydra config — data, cache, alembic, etc.
427	│   ├── constants/                       # optional — enums / dataclasses shared across layers
428	│   ├── data_layers/
429	│   │   ├── data/
430	│   │   │   └── db/
431	│   │   │       ├── entities/            # SQLAlchemy ORM classes (one file per table)
432	│   │   │       └── migrations/
433	│   │   │           ├── env.py
434	│   │   │           ├── script.py.mako
435	│   │   │           └── versions/        # generated migration files
436	│   │   ├── data_access/                 # thin DB query classes (one per entity or domain group)
437	│   │   └── service/                     # business logic, caching, ResultToDict
438	│   ├── jobs/                            # background tasks (optional)
439	│   ├── client/                          # outbound HTTP / external-service clients (optional)
440	│   └── <name>_core_lib.py               # the main `class YourCoreLib(CoreLib):` — composition root
441	├── hydra_plugins/<name>_core_lib/       # Hydra plugin that advertises this lib's config to parents
442	├── tests/
443	│   └── data/config/                     # test-specific YAML overrides
444	├── setup.py / pyproject.toml
445	└── requirements.txt
446	```
447	
448	Verify against `form-core-lib/` and `ob-love-admin-backend/admin_core_lib/` — both match.
449	
450	## The three data layers
451	
452	### 1. Data — `data_layers/data/db/entities/`
453	SQLAlchemy models only. Columns, relationships, mixins.
454	
455	```python
456	from core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.base import Base
457	from core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_mixin import SoftDeleteMixin
458	from core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_token_mixin import SoftDeleteTokenMixin
459	
460	class User(Base, SoftDeleteMixin, SoftDeleteTokenMixin):
461	    __tablename__ = 'user'
462	    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
463	    email = Column(VARCHAR(255), nullable=False)
464	```
465	
466	`SoftDeleteMixin` → `created_at`, `updated_at`, `deleted_at`. `SoftDeleteTokenMixin` → `deleted_at_token` (int; 0 = active, non-zero = ms timestamp). The token exists because indexing a DateTime is slow.
467	
468	### 2. DataAccess — `data_layers/data_access/`
469	Queries only. Extends one of:
470	
471	| Class | Hard delete? | Required mixins | Use when |
472	|---|---|---|---|
473	| `CRUD` | abstract | — | you need custom get/delete logic |
474	| `CRUDDataAccess` | yes | — | plain CRUD, hard deletes |
475	| `CRUDSoftDeleteDataAccess` | no | `SoftDeleteMixin` | `get()` filters `deleted_at IS NULL`; `delete()` sets it |
476	| `CRUDSoftDeleteWithTokenDataAccess` | no | both mixins | same + indexed `deleted_at_token` lookup |
477	
478	Constructor: `__init__(self, db_entity, db: SqlAlchemyConnectionFactory, rule_validator: RuleValidator = None)`.
479	
480	### 3. Service — `data_layers/service/`
481	Business logic, caching, transformation. Extends `core_lib.data_layers.service.service.Service`.
482	
483	```python
484	CACHE_KEY_USER = 'key_user_{user_id}'
485	
486	class UserService(Service):
487	    def __init__(self, user_data_access: UserDataAccess):
488	        self._user_data_access = user_data_access
489	
490	    @Cache(CACHE_KEY_USER)
491	    @ResultToDict()
492	    def get(self, user_id: int):
493	        return self._user_data_access.get(user_id)
494	```
495	
496	**Decorator order matters.** `@Cache` *outside* `@ResultToDict`. Memcached/Redis cache handlers only accept dicts and lists — always serialize before caching.
497	
498	## Main class — `<name>_core_lib.py`
499	
500	```python
501	class FormCoreLib(CoreLib):
502	    def __init__(self, config: DictConfig):
503	        CoreLib.__init__(self)     # required — wires event listeners
504	        self.config = config
505	        db = instantiate_config(self.config.core_lib.data.db, SqlAlchemyConnectionFactory)
506	        self.form_page_field = FormPageFieldService(FormPageFieldDataAccess(db))
507	
508	    @staticmethod
509	    def install(cfg):   Alembic(os.path.dirname(inspect.getfile(FormCoreLib)), cfg).upgrade()
510	    @staticmethod
511	    def uninstall(cfg): Alembic(os.path.dirname(inspect.getfile(FormCoreLib)), cfg).downgrade()
512	```
513	
514	Two rules:
515	1. Always call `CoreLib.__init__(self)` first.
516	2. Public attributes are the library's API surface. Callers outside the core-lib should only go through `core_lib.some_service.some_method(...)` — never into `DataAccess` directly.
517	
518	### Singleton accessor
519	
520	```python
521	class FormCoreLibInstance:
522	    _app = None
523	    @staticmethod
524	    def init(cfg):
525	        if FormCoreLibInstance._app is None:
526	            FormCoreLibInstance._app = FormCoreLib(cfg)
527	    @staticmethod
528	    def get() -> FormCoreLib:
529	        return FormCoreLibInstance._app
530	```
531	
532	## Config (Hydra / YAML)
533	
534	Each core-lib ships `config/<name>_core_lib.yaml`. Parent apps override sections they care about.
535	
536	```yaml
537	core_lib:
538	  data:
539	    sqlalchemy:
540	      log_queries: false
541	      create_db: true
542	      session: { pool_recycle: 3600, pool_pre_ping: false }
543	      url: { protocol: sqlite }
544	alembic:
545	  version_table: <name>_alembic_version        # MUST be unique per core-lib
546	  script_location: data_layers/data/db/migrations
547	  sqlalchemy.url: ${core_lib.data}
548	  file_template: "%%(year)d-%%(month).2d-%%(day).2d_%%(rev)s_%%(slug)s"
549	  version_file_name: '.migration_ver'
550	```
551	
552	Apps load config via `@hydra.main(config_path='.', config_name='<name>_core_lib.yaml')`. Tests use `hydra.initialize(config_path=...)` + `hydra.compose(...)`.
553	
554	## Migrations
555	
556	- Location: `data_layers/data/db/migrations/versions/`.
557	- `version_table` must be unique per core-lib so composed libs don't collide.
558	- File naming: `YYYY-MM-DD_<rev>_<slug>.py`.
559	- Run programmatically: `YourCoreLib.install(cfg)` / `uninstall(cfg)` (wraps Alembic `upgrade` / `downgrade`).
560	- Scaffold a new core-lib: `core_lib -g ExampleCoreLib.yaml`.
561	- **`.migration_ver` (`data_layers/data/db/migrations/.migration_ver`) is the head-revision pin** — its single line must equal the highest `revision` string under `versions/`. Whenever you add a new migration, bump this file in the same commit. The filename comes from the `version_file_name` Hydra setting in `<name>_core_lib.yaml`.
562	
563	## Cross-cutting utilities
564	
565	| Import | What it does |
566	|---|---|
567	| `core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.base.Base` | SQLAlchemy declarative base for entities. |
568	| `core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_mixin.SoftDeleteMixin` | Adds `created_at` / `updated_at` / `deleted_at`. |
569	| `core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_token_mixin.SoftDeleteTokenMixin` | Adds indexed `deleted_at_token` (int, 0 = active). |
570	| `core_lib.connection.sql_alchemy_connection_factory.SqlAlchemyConnectionFactory` | `with factory.get() as session:` context manager. Mongo / Solr / Neo4j variants exist. |
571	| `core_lib.data_transform.result_to_dict.ResultToDict` (decorator) / `result_to_dict` (function) | Turns SQLAlchemy rows, objects, enums, datetimes (→ timestamp) into plain dicts. |
572	| `core_lib.cache.cache_decorator.Cache` | `@Cache(key, expire, invalidate, handler_name)`. Key supports `{param_name}`. |
573	| `core_lib.cache.cache_registry.CacheRegistry` | Holds named `CacheHandler` instances; `@Cache(handler_name=...)` resolves here. |
574	| `core_lib.web_helpers.decorators.HandleException` | View decorator; `StatusCodeException → mapped status`, others → 500. |
575	| `core_lib.error_handling.status_code_exception.StatusCodeException` | Raise with an `HTTPStatus` to have `@HandleException` return that status. |
576	| `core_lib.rule_validator.rule_validator.RuleValidator` | Optional; validates data passed into `create()` / `update()`. |
577	| `core_lib.web_helpers.request_response_helpers` | `request_body_dict`, `response_ok`, `response_json`, `response_status`. |
578	| `core_lib.web_helpers.web_helpers_utils.WebHelpersUtils` | Call `.init(ServerType.Flask | Django)` once at boot. |
579	| `core_lib.helpers.config_instances.instantiate_config` | Build an instance from a Hydra `DictConfig` node. |
580	| `core_lib.data_layers.service.service.Service` | Base class for every service. |
581	| `core_lib.alembic.alembic.Alembic` | `.upgrade()`, `.downgrade()`, `.history()`, `.create_migration(name)`. |
582	
583	### CacheHandler implementations shipped
584	
585	| Class | Notes |
586	|---|---|
587	| `CacheHandlerRam` | In-memory. Lost on process exit. |
588	| `CacheHandlerMemcached` | External memcached. Only serializes `dict` / `list`. |
589	| `CacheHandlerRedis` | External redis. Same dict/list-only constraint. |
590	| `CacheHandlerNoCache` | Dummy; for tests. |
591	
592	## Testing
593	
594	```python
595	from hydra.core.global_hydra import GlobalHydra
596	import hydra, os, unittest
597	
598	def get_config():
599	    GlobalHydra.instance().clear()
600	    hydra.initialize(config_path=os.path.join('..', 'data', 'config'))
601	    return hydra.compose('config.yaml')
602	
603	class TestForm(unittest.TestCase):
604	    def setUp(self):
605	        self.core_lib = FormCoreLib(get_config())
606	```
607	
608	Test-only entities live in `tests/data/entities/` and are created via `TestModel.__table__.create(engine, checkfirst=True)` in a helper. See `form-core-lib/tests/` for the reference shape.
609	
610	## Cheat sheet — starting work in a new `*-core-lib`
611	
612	1. `ls <repo>/<name>_core_lib/` — expect `config/`, maybe `constants/`, `data_layers/`, `<name>_core_lib.py`.
613	2. Open `<name>_core_lib.py` (the composition root) — it maps which services wrap which data_accesses.
614	3. Entities → `data_layers/data/db/entities/`. Queries → `data_layers/data_access/`. Business logic → `data_layers/service/`.
615	4. Never call a `DataAccess` from outside the library — go through `Service`.
616	5. When adding a table: entity → data_access → service → wire into main class → Alembic migration → test.
617	6. Column-alias discipline in `JoinConfig` / joined queries: every joined column gets a unique `.label('owner_column_name')`. Same-named columns silently overwrite in `result_to_dict`.

file.content:
## `interest_core_lib` — user-interest catalog / matchmaking enrichment

**What it does.** Wraps external catalog APIs (TMDB movie+TV, Spotify, Google Books, Wikiart) plus an internal Solr-backed core-values/activities catalog behind one uniform "interest item" shape. Used to enrich a user's declared interests with structured, matchmaking-relevant attributes (genres, cast/creators, audio features, etc.).

**Key modules.**
- `interest_core_lib/interest_core_lib.py` — composition root; registers handlers from Hydra config, owns the Solr client + cache.
- `interest_core_lib/data_layers/service/interest_service.py` — `InterestService.search/get/suggestions` with `@Cache` + `handle_reconnect` (one retry that reconnects the handler, then maps to `StatusCodeException`).
- `interest_core_lib/client/interest_handler.py` — `InterestHandler` ABC. Concrete handlers under `client/<domain>/`; test doubles under `mocks/`.

**The "interest item" contract (hidden, test-enforced).** Every `InterestHandler.to_interest_item(...)` must return a dict with the common base keys (`external_id`, `label`, `description`, `image_url`, `thumbnail_url`, `preview_url`, `url`, `source`, `creator`, `meta_data`) plus domain-specific matchmaking fields. There is **no shared schema class** — the key contract lives in each domain's `tests/<domain>_test.py` `keys_list`, asserted via `assertIn(key, item)` against **live API calls** (real config keys required; not run in offline CI). When you add a field to a handler's `to_interest_item`, add it to that domain's `keys_list` too. `books_test.py` is the exception — it only prints, no `keys_list`. `tests/interest_data_test.py` complements those: it is **offline and deterministic** (fakes the network/SDK calls) and asserts the extracted *values* — including the movie-vs-TV / search-vs-info dual-path fallbacks and the graceful-degradation failure paths — so it runs in CI without credentials and is the place to add value-level assertions for new fields.

**Dual call path — `to_interest_item` runs on two differently-shaped inputs.** `InterestHandler.search()` calls `to_interest_item` on each *search-result* element; `InterestHandler.info()` calls `get_full_info()` then `to_interest_item` on the *full-info* object. Handlers must read fields defensively because the same method sees both shapes (e.g. TMDB movie vs TV: `release_date`/`original_title` vs `first_air_date`/`original_name`; TMDB `_to_interest_search_result` always re-fetches via `_get_full_info` so cast/genres/keywords work on both paths). Extra per-item enrichment calls (TMDB keywords, Spotify artist genres) are wrapped in try/except + `logger.warning` so one failing sub-call never drops the whole item.

**Depends on / consumed by.** Depends on `core-lib` (Service/Cache/Solr/ClientBase) and external APIs via `tmdbsimple`/`spotipy`/HTTP. Standalone `*-core-lib`; follows the framework conventions below (Hydra config at `config/interest_core_lib.yaml`, onion layers, singleton-style main class). No owned SQL tables — its catalog store is Solr.

---

# core-lib framework conventions

Every `*-core-lib` package in this workspace (present or future) follows the layout and idioms below. This section is stable — the details come straight from `core-lib/docs/pages/core_lib_doc/`.

## Philosophy

- **Onion Architecture.** Three concentric layers: `data → data_access → service`. Outer layers may call inner; never the reverse. Business logic and caching belong in `service`, DB access in `data_access`, entities/connectors in `data`.
- **POPO** (Plain Old Python Object). No framework lock-in. A core-lib should run headless in tests and be pluggable into Flask, Django, or anything else.
- **Composable.** A core-lib is a plugin. Embed another via `instantiate_config(self.config, OtherCoreLib)` from `core_lib.helpers.config_instances`.
- **Loose coupling via Hydra.** All wiring is YAML-driven; config is passed in as `DictConfig`.

## Canonical folder tree

```
<name>-core-lib/                         # project directory (the git repo)
├── <name>_core_lib/                     # main code package (underscored)
│   ├── config/
│   │   └── <name>_core_lib.yaml         # Hydra config — data, cache, alembic, etc.
│   ├── constants/                       # optional — enums / dataclasses shared across layers
│   ├── data_layers/
│   │   ├── data/
│   │   │   └── db/
│   │   │       ├── entities/            # SQLAlchemy ORM classes (one file per table)
│   │   │       └── migrations/
│   │   │           ├── env.py
│   │   │           ├── script.py.mako
│   │   │           └── versions/        # generated migration files
│   │   ├── data_access/                 # thin DB query classes (one per entity or domain group)
│   │   └── service/                     # business logic, caching, ResultToDict
│   ├── jobs/                            # background tasks (optional)
│   ├── client/                          # outbound HTTP / external-service clients (optional)
│   └── <name>_core_lib.py               # the main `class YourCoreLib(CoreLib):` — composition root
├── hydra_plugins/<name>_core_lib/       # Hydra plugin that advertises this lib's config to parents
├── tests/
│   └── data/config/                     # test-specific YAML overrides
├── setup.py / pyproject.toml
└── requirements.txt
```

Verify against `form-core-lib/` and `ob-love-admin-backend/admin_core_lib/` — both match.

## The three data layers

### 1. Data — `data_layers/data/db/entities/`
SQLAlchemy models only. Columns, relationships, mixins.

```python
from core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.base import Base
from core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_mixin import SoftDeleteMixin
from core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_token_mixin import SoftDeleteTokenMixin

class User(Base, SoftDeleteMixin, SoftDeleteTokenMixin):
    __tablename__ = 'user'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    email = Column(VARCHAR(255), nullable=False)
```

`SoftDeleteMixin` → `created_at`, `updated_at`, `deleted_at`. `SoftDeleteTokenMixin` → `deleted_at_token` (int; 0 = active, non-zero = ms timestamp). The token exists because indexing a DateTime is slow.

### 2. DataAccess — `data_layers/data_access/`
Queries only. Extends one of:

| Class | Hard delete? | Required mixins | Use when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CRUD` | abstract | — | you need custom get/delete logic |
| `CRUDDataAccess` | yes | — | plain CRUD, hard deletes |
| `CRUDSoftDeleteDataAccess` | no | `SoftDeleteMixin` | `get()` filters `deleted_at IS NULL`; `delete()` sets it |
| `CRUDSoftDeleteWithTokenDataAccess` | no | both mixins | same + indexed `deleted_at_token` lookup |

Constructor: `__init__(self, db_entity, db: SqlAlchemyConnectionFactory, rule_validator: RuleValidator = None)`.

### 3. Service — `data_layers/service/`
Business logic, caching, transformation. Extends `core_lib.data_layers.service.service.Service`.

```python
CACHE_KEY_USER = 'key_user_{user_id}'

class UserService(Service):
    def __init__(self, user_data_access: UserDataAccess):
        self._user_data_access = user_data_access

    @Cache(CACHE_KEY_USER)
    @ResultToDict()
    def get(self, user_id: int):
        return self._user_data_access.get(user_id)
```

**Decorator order matters.** `@Cache` *outside* `@ResultToDict`. Memcached/Redis cache handlers only accept dicts and lists — always serialize before caching.

## Main class — `<name>_core_lib.py`

```python
class FormCoreLib(CoreLib):
    def __init__(self, config: DictConfig):
        CoreLib.__init__(self)     # required — wires event listeners
        self.config = config
        db = instantiate_config(self.config.core_lib.data.db, SqlAlchemyConnectionFactory)
        self.form_page_field = FormPageFieldService(FormPageFieldDataAccess(db))

    @staticmethod
    def install(cfg):   Alembic(os.path.dirname(inspect.getfile(FormCoreLib)), cfg).upgrade()
    @staticmethod
    def uninstall(cfg): Alembic(os.path.dirname(inspect.getfile(FormCoreLib)), cfg).downgrade()
```

Two rules:
1. Always call `CoreLib.__init__(self)` first.
2. Public attributes are the library's API surface. Callers outside the core-lib should only go through `core_lib.some_service.some_method(...)` — never into `DataAccess` directly.

### Singleton accessor

```python
class FormCoreLibInstance:
    _app = None
    @staticmethod
    def init(cfg):
        if FormCoreLibInstance._app is None:
            FormCoreLibInstance._app = FormCoreLib(cfg)
    @staticmethod
    def get() -> FormCoreLib:
        return FormCoreLibInstance._app
```

## Config (Hydra / YAML)

Each core-lib ships `config/<name>_core_lib.yaml`. Parent apps override sections they care about.

```yaml
core_lib:
  data:
    sqlalchemy:
      log_queries: false
      create_db: true
      session: { pool_recycle: 3600, pool_pre_ping: false }
      url: { protocol: sqlite }
alembic:
  version_table: <name>_alembic_version        # MUST be unique per core-lib
  script_location: data_layers/data/db/migrations
  sqlalchemy.url: ${core_lib.data}
  file_template: "%%(year)d-%%(month).2d-%%(day).2d_%%(rev)s_%%(slug)s"
  version_file_name: '.migration_ver'
```

Apps load config via `@hydra.main(config_path='.', config_name='<name>_core_lib.yaml')`. Tests use `hydra.initialize(config_path=...)` + `hydra.compose(...)`.

## Migrations

- Location: `data_layers/data/db/migrations/versions/`.
- `version_table` must be unique per core-lib so composed libs don't collide.
- File naming: `YYYY-MM-DD_<rev>_<slug>.py`.
- Run programmatically: `YourCoreLib.install(cfg)` / `uninstall(cfg)` (wraps Alembic `upgrade` / `downgrade`).
- Scaffold a new core-lib: `core_lib -g ExampleCoreLib.yaml`.
- **`.migration_ver` (`data_layers/data/db/migrations/.migration_ver`) is the head-revision pin** — its single line must equal the highest `revision` string under `versions/`. Whenever you add a new migration, bump this file in the same commit. The filename comes from the `version_file_name` Hydra setting in `<name>_core_lib.yaml`.

## Cross-cutting utilities

| Import | What it does |
|---|---|
| `core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.base.Base` | SQLAlchemy declarative base for entities. |
| `core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_mixin.SoftDeleteMixin` | Adds `created_at` / `updated_at` / `deleted_at`. |
| `core_lib.data_layers.data.db.sqlalchemy.mixins.soft_delete_token_mixin.SoftDeleteTokenMixin` | Adds indexed `deleted_at_token` (int, 0 = active). |
| `core_lib.connection.sql_alchemy_connection_factory.SqlAlchemyConnectionFactory` | `with factory.get() as session:` context manager. Mongo / Solr / Neo4j variants exist. |
| `core_lib.data_transform.result_to_dict.ResultToDict` (decorator) / `result_to_dict` (function) | Turns SQLAlchemy rows, objects, enums, datetimes (→ timestamp) into plain dicts. |
| `core_lib.cache.cache_decorator.Cache` | `@Cache(key, expire, invalidate, handler_name)`. Key supports `{param_name}`. |
| `core_lib.cache.cache_registry.CacheRegistry` | Holds named `CacheHandler` instances; `@Cache(handler_name=...)` resolves here. |
| `core_lib.web_helpers.decorators.HandleException` | View decorator; `StatusCodeException → mapped status`, others → 500. |
| `core_lib.error_handling.status_code_exception.StatusCodeException` | Raise with an `HTTPStatus` to have `@HandleException` return that status. |
| `core_lib.rule_validator.rule_validator.RuleValidator` | Optional; validates data passed into `create()` / `update()`. |
| `core_lib.web_helpers.request_response_helpers` | `request_body_dict`, `response_ok`, `response_json`, `response_status`. |
| `core_lib.web_helpers.web_helpers_utils.WebHelpersUtils` | Call `.init(ServerType.Flask | Django)` once at boot. |
| `core_lib.helpers.config_instances.instantiate_config` | Build an instance from a Hydra `DictConfig` node. |
| `core_lib.data_layers.service.service.Service` | Base class for every service. |
| `core_lib.alembic.alembic.Alembic` | `.upgrade()`, `.downgrade()`, `.history()`, `.create_migration(name)`. |

### CacheHandler implementations shipped

| Class | Notes |
|---|---|
| `CacheHandlerRam` | In-memory. Lost on process exit. |
| `CacheHandlerMemcached` | External memcached. Only serializes `dict` / `list`. |
| `CacheHandlerRedis` | External redis. Same dict/list-only constraint. |
| `CacheHandlerNoCache` | Dummy; for tests. |

## Testing

```python
from hydra.core.global_hydra import GlobalHydra
import hydra, os, unittest

def get_config():
    GlobalHydra.instance().clear()
    hydra.initialize(config_path=os.path.join('..', 'data', 'config'))
    return hydra.compose('config.yaml')

class TestForm(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.core_lib = FormCoreLib(get_config())
```

Test-only entities live in `tests/data/entities/` and are created via `TestModel.__table__.create(engine, checkfirst=True)` in a helper. See `form-core-lib/tests/` for the reference shape.

## Cheat sheet — starting work in a new `*-core-lib`

1. `ls <repo>/<name>_core_lib/` — expect `config/`, maybe `constants/`, `data_layers/`, `<name>_core_lib.py`.
2. Open `<name>_core_lib.py` (the composition root) — it maps which services wrap which data_accesses.
3. Entities → `data_layers/data/db/entities/`. Queries → `data_layers/data_access/`. Business logic → `data_layers/service/`.
4. Never call a `DataAccess` from outside the library — go through `Service`.
5. When adding a table: entity → data_access → service → wire into main class → Alembic migration → test.
6. Column-alias discipline in `JoinConfig` / joined queries: every joined column gets a unique `.label('owner_column_name')`. Same-named columns silently overwrite in `result_to_dict`.

1	import logging
2	import os

4	from spotipy import Spotify, SpotifyClientCredentials
5	
6	from interest_core_lib.client.interest_handler import InterestHandler
7	from interest_core_lib.constants import CLIENT_TIMEOUT
8	
9	
10	def images_links_compare_desc(item):
11	    return int(item['height'])
12

14	class SpotifyMusicInterestHandler(InterestHandler):
15	
16	    def __init__(self, client_id: str, client_secret: str):
17	        InterestHandler.__init__(self)
18	        self.logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
19	        self._client_id = client_id
20	        self._client_secret = client_secret
21	        self._sp = None
22	        self.icon_path_str = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'spotify.png')
23	
24	    def connect(self):
25	        self._sp = Spotify(client_credentials_manager=SpotifyClientCredentials(client_id=self._client_id, client_secret=self._client_secret))
26	
27	    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
28	        return self._sp.search(term)['tracks'].get('items')
29	
30	    def to_interest_item(self, item: dict):
31	        album = item['album']
32	        images = sorted(album.get('images') or [], key=images_links_compare_desc)
33	        image_url = None
34	        thumbnail_url = None
35	        creator = ', '.join([a['name'] for a in item.get('artists')])
36	        preview_url = item.get('preview_url')
37	        if images:

import logging
import os

from spotipy import Spotify, SpotifyClientCredentials

from interest_core_lib.client.interest_handler import InterestHandler
from interest_core_lib.constants import CLIENT_TIMEOUT


def images_links_compare_desc(item):
    return int(item['height'])


class SpotifyMusicInterestHandler(InterestHandler):

    def __init__(self, client_id: str, client_secret: str):
        InterestHandler.__init__(self)
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
        self._client_id = client_id
        self._client_secret = client_secret
        self._sp = None
        self.icon_path_str = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'spotify.png')

    def connect(self):
        self._sp = Spotify(client_credentials_manager=SpotifyClientCredentials(client_id=self._client_id, client_secret=self._client_secret))

    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
        return self._sp.search(term)['tracks'].get('items')

    def to_interest_item(self, item: dict):
        album = item['album']
        images = sorted(album.get('images') or [], key=images_links_compare_desc)
        image_url = None
        thumbnail_url = None
        creator = ', '.join([a['name'] for a in item.get('artists')])
        preview_url = item.get('preview_url')
        if images:
            images_len = len(images)
            thumbnail_url = images[images_len - 2 if images_len > 1 else 0]['url']
            image_url = images[images_len - 1]['url']

        track_info = self.get_full_info(item['id'])
        audio_features = self._sp.audio_features([item['id']])[0]
        genres = []
        try:
            artists = item.get('artists') or []
            if artists:
                genres = self._sp.artist(artists[0]['id']).get('genres', [])
        except Exception:
            self.logger.warning('unable to load artist genres, id: `{}`'.format(item.get('id')))
        return {
            'external_id': item['id'],
            'label': item['name'],
            'description': album.get('name') or '',
            'url': item['external_urls']['spotify'],
            'image_url': image_url,
            'thumbnail_url': thumbnail_url,
            'preview_url': preview_url,
            'source': 'spotify',
            'creator': creator,
            'meta_data': {},
            'genres': genres,
            'popularity': track_info.get('popularity'),
            'release_year': (track_info['album']['release_date']).split('-')[0],
            'explicit': item.get('explicit'),
            'album_type': album.get('album_type'),
            'danceability': audio_features.get('danceability'),
            'energy': audio_features.get('energy'),
            'speechiness': audio_features.get('speechiness'),
            'tempo': audio_features.get('tempo'),
            'acousticness': audio_features.get('acousticness'),
            'instrumentalness': audio_features.get('instrumentalness'),
            'valence': audio_features.get('valence'),
            'loudness': audio_features.get('loudness'),
            'liveness': audio_features.get('liveness'),
            'key': audio_features.get('key'),
            'mode': audio_features.get('mode'),
            'time_signature': audio_features.get('time_signature'),
            'duration_ms': audio_features.get('duration_ms'),
        }

    def icon_path(self):
        return self.icon_path_str

    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
        return self._sp.track('spotify:track:{}'.format(external_id))

import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def to_interest_search_result(result, is_movie: bool):
    creator = ''
    if 'created_by' in result:
        try:
            creator = ', '.join([c['name'] for c in result.get('created_by')])
        except Exception as ex:
            logger.error('error while loading creator, id: `{}`m is_movie: `{}`'.format(result.get('id'), is_movie))
            logger.exception(ex)

    return {
        'external_id': result['id'],
        'label': result.get('name') or result.get('title'),
        'description': result['overview'],
        'image_url': 'https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/original/{}'.format(result['poster_path']),
        'thumbnail_url': 'https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/original/{}'.format(result['poster_path']),
        'url': 'https://www.themoviedb.org/{}/{}'.format('movie' if is_movie else 'tv', result['id']),
        'source': 'tmdb',
        'creator': creator,
        'meta_data': {},
    }

import logging
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod


class InterestHandler(ABC):

    def __init__(self, max_results: int = None):
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
        self._is_connected = False
        self.max_results = max_results

    def search(self, term: str):
        search_results = self.to_search_results(term)
        result = []
        max_results = self.max_results if self.max_results and self.max_results > 0 else None
        for index, item in enumerate(search_results):
            if max_results and index >= max_results:
                break
            result.append(self.to_interest_item(item))
        return result

    def info(self, external_id):
        full_info = self.get_full_info(external_id)
        if not full_info:
            raise ValueError('get_full_info of:`{}`. cannot be null, external_id: `{}`'.format(self.__class__.__qualname__, external_id))
        return self.to_interest_item(full_info)

    def is_connected(self):
        return self._is_connected

    def connect_handler(self):
        try:
            self.connect()
        except BaseException as e:
            self._is_connected = False
            self.logger.error(e)
            raise e

    @abstractmethod
    def connect(self):
        """
        Connect to the interest datasource, to be able to fetch data,
        you should raise an exception when connection failed.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
        """
        Will search the interest service according to the term parameter,
        and fetch the search results from the network as is without manipulation

        :param term: the search term to search by
        :return: a list of results according to the service format un touched.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def to_interest_item(self, data: dict):
        """
        Will convert the service interest data to the same data interest_item data structure.
        :param data: the data fetched from the interest service
        :return: interest_item common data structure
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
        """
        Fetch the full information from the interest data service
        :param external_id: The external_id of the interest in the data interest service format
        :return: converted data interest format into common interest_item data structure
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def icon_path(self):
        """
        Will get the interest service provider icon path
        :return: Path to the interest service provider
        """
        pass

    def suggestions(self, ids: list):
        """
        Will return a suggestion list to show on the screen, so the user will not need to search.
        :param ids: the ids of items to fetch
        :return: return interest_item data structure for the suggestions
        """
        raise ValueError('Not supported for this interest type')

    def close(self):
        """
        on termination of the running application it will close all interest handlers
        :return:
        """
        pass

import logging
import os
from http import HTTPStatus

from core_lib.client.client_base import ClientBase
from core_lib.error_handling.status_code_exception import StatusCodeException
from interest_core_lib.client.interest_handler import InterestHandler
from interest_core_lib.constants import CLIENT_TIMEOUT

sizes = ['smallThumbnail', 'thumbnail', 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'extraLarge']


def images_links_compare_desc(item):
    item_index = sizes.index(item)
    if item_index == -1:
        item_index = len(sizes)
    return len(sizes) - item_index


class GoogleBooksInterestHandler(ClientBase, InterestHandler):

    def __init__(self):
        InterestHandler.__init__(self)
        ClientBase.__init__(self, 'https://www.googleapis.com')
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
        self.set_timeout(CLIENT_TIMEOUT)
        self.icon_path_str = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'google_books.png')

    def connect(self):
        pass

    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
        return self._get('books/v1/volumes', params={'q': term}).json().get('items', [])

    def to_interest_item(self, data: dict):
        if 'error' in data:
            status = data.get('error').get('code') or HTTPStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
            self.logger.error('book information contains error, can\'t find it')
            raise StatusCodeException(status, 'book information contains error, can\'t find it')

        volume_info = data['volumeInfo']
        images_dict = volume_info.get('imageLinks') or {}
        # links = sorted(list(images_dict.keys()), key=images_links_compare_desc)
        image_url = images_dict.get('thumbnail') or images_dict.get('smallThumbnail')
        if image_url:
            image_url = image_url.replace('http://', 'https://')
        else:
            self.logger.warning(f'missing image_url for id {data.get("id")}')
        # image_url = None
        # if links:
        #     image_url = images_dict.get(links[0])

        creator_list = volume_info.get('authors')
        creator = ''
        if isinstance(creator_list, list):
            creator = ', '.join(creator_list)
        elif isinstance(creator_list, str):
            creator = creator_list

        if 'volumeInfo' not in data and 'title' not in data.get('volumeInfo'):
            self.logger.error('"volumeInfo" not found in data')
            raise StatusCodeException(HTTPStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, f'"volumeInfo" not found in data for to_interest_item: {data.get("id")}')

        published_date = volume_info.get('publishedDate')
        return {
            'external_id': data['id'],
            'label': volume_info.get('title'),
            'description': volume_info.get('description') or volume_info.get('subtitle') or '',
            'image_url': image_url,
            'thumbnail_url': image_url,
            'preview_url': None,
            'url': volume_info.get('previewLink'),
            'source': 'google_books',
            'creator': creator,
            'meta_data': {},
            'genres': volume_info.get('categories', []),
            'release_year': published_date.split('-')[0] if published_date else None,
            'language': volume_info.get('language'),
            'page_count': volume_info.get('pageCount'),
            'user_rating': volume_info.get('averageRating'),
            'ratings_count': volume_info.get('ratingsCount'),
            'publisher': volume_info.get('publisher'),
            'maturity_rating': volume_info.get('maturityRating'),
            'is_series': 'seriesInfo' in volume_info,
        }

    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
        return self._get('books/v1/volumes/{}'.format(external_id)).json()

    def icon_path(self):
        return self.icon_path_str

    def close(self):
        self.session.close()

import logging
import os
from contextlib import suppress
from http import HTTPStatus
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from core_lib.client.client_base import ClientBase
from core_lib.error_handling.status_code_exception import StatusCodeException
from interest_core_lib.client.interest_handler import InterestHandler
from interest_core_lib.constants import CLIENT_TIMEOUT

filter_image_urls = ['https://uploads.wikiart.org/Content/images/FRAME-600x480.jpg']


class WikiartInterestHandler(ClientBase, InterestHandler):

    def __init__(self, client_id: str, client_secret: str, max_results: int = 20):
        ClientBase.__init__(self, 'https://www.wikiart.org/en/api/2')
        InterestHandler.__init__(self, max_results)

        self.logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
        self._client_id = client_id
        self._client_secret = client_secret
        self.set_timeout(CLIENT_TIMEOUT)
        self.token = None
        self.icon_path_str = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'wikiart.png')

    def _build_url(self, artistId, image_url):
        file_base_name = os.path.basename(urlparse(image_url).path)
        return 'https://www.wikiart.org/en/{}/{}'.format(artistId, os.path.splitext(file_base_name)[0])

    def connect(self):
        result = self._post('login?accessCode={}&secretCode={}'.format(self._client_id, self._client_secret))
        if result.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK:
            self.token = result.json().get('SessionKey')
        else:
            raise ValueError(f'Enable to get token for WikiartInterestHandler, {result.content.decode("utf-8")}')

    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
        url = 'PaintingSearch?term={}&authSessionKey={}'.format(term, self.token)
        r = self._get(url).json().get('data')
        return self._filter_results(r)

    def _filter_results(self, results):
        return filter(lambda result: result and result.get('image') not in filter_image_urls, results)

    def to_interest_item(self, data: dict):
        if 'Status' in data and data.get('Status') == 404:
            raise StatusCodeException(HTTPStatus.NOT_ACCEPTABLE)

        image_url = data.get('image')
        with suppress(Exception):
            image_url = image_url[0:image_url.index('!')]

        full_info = self.get_full_info(data['id'])
        return {
            'external_id': data['id'],
            'label': data['title'],
            'description': 'Artist Name: {}, Year:{}'.format(data.get('artistName') or '',
                                                             data.get('completitionYear') or ''),
            'url': data.get('url') or self._build_url(data['artistId'], image_url),
            'image_url': image_url,
            'thumbnail_url': image_url,
            'preview_url': None,
            'source': 'wikiart',
            'creator': data.get('artistName'),
            'meta_data': {},
            'genres': full_info.get('genres', []),
            'styles': full_info.get('styles', []),
            'period': full_info.get('period'),
            'tags': full_info.get('tags', []),
            'location': full_info.get('location'),
            'media': full_info.get('media', []),
            'release_year': data.get('completitionYear') or full_info.get('completitionYear') or full_info.get('yearAsString'),
            'serie': full_info.get('serie'),
        }

    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
        return self._get('Painting?id={}'.format(external_id)).json()

    def icon_path(self):
        return self.icon_path_str

    def close(self):
        self.session.close()

10	        InterestHandler.__init__(self)
11	        self._interest_type = interest_type
12	        self._solr = None
13	        self.icon_path_str = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'una.png')
14

16	        pass
17	
18	    def set_solr_client(self, solr_client: SolrConnectionFactory):
19	        self._solr = solr_client
20	
21	    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
22	        if not term:
23	            return []
24	        with self._solr.get() as session:
25	            query = {'rows': 20}
26	            term_search = '*'
27	            for ch in term:
28	                term_search = term_search + ch + '*'
29	
30	            filter_queries = [
31	                'interest_type:{}'.format(self._interest_type),
32	                'interest_values:{}'.format(term_search)
33	            ]
34	            query['fq'] = filter_queries
35	            return session.search("*:*", **query)
36	
37	    def to_interest_item(self, data: dict):
38	        return {
39	            'external_id': data['interest_id'],
40	            'label': data['interest_id'],
41	            'description': '',
42	            'image_url': '{}.png'.format(data['interest_id']),
43	            'thumbnail_url': '{}.png'.format(data['interest_id']),
44	            'preview_url': None,
45	            'url': '',
46	            'source': 'interest_core_lib',
47	            'creator': '',
48	            'meta_data': {'color': data['color']},
49	        }
50	
51	    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
52	        with self._solr.get() as session:
53	            query = {'fq': ['interest_id:{}'.format(external_id),
54	                            'interest_type:{}'.format(self._interest_type)],
55	                     'rows': 20}
56	            result = session.search("*:*", **query)
57	            if result.docs:
58	                return dict(result.docs[0])
59	
60	    def close(self):
61	        pass
62	        # print(f'SolrInterestHandler.close {self._interest_type}')
63	        # traceback.print_stack()
64	    #     if self._solr:
65	    #         with self._solr.get() as solr:
66	    #             solr.get_session().close()
67	
68	    def icon_path(self):
69	        return self.icon_path_str
70	
71	    def suggestions(self, ids: list):
72	        with self._solr.get() as session:
73	            query = {'rows': 250}
74	            filter_queries = [f'interest_type:{self._interest_type}']
75	            if ids and len(ids) > 0:
76	                ids_str = ' '.join(ids)
77	                filter_queries.append(f'interest_id:({ids_str})')
78	
79	            query['fq'] = filter_queries
80	            query['sort'] = 'interest_id asc'
81	            return [self.to_interest_item(item) for item in session.search("*:*", **query)]
82

import unittest

from interest_core_lib.client.tmdb.tmdb_v4_interest_handler import TMDBv4InterestHandler
from tests.helpers import load_config

cfg = load_config()

keys_list = [
    'external_id',
    'label',
    'description',
    'image_url',
    'thumbnail_url',
    'url',
    'preview_url',
    'source',
    'creator',
    'meta_data',
    'popularity',
    'language',
    'release_year',
    'cast',
    'directors',
    'writers',
    'genres',
    'keywords',
    'runtime',
    'user_rating',
    'vote_count',
    'original_title',
    'production_countries',
]

class TMDBTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_movie(self):
        client = TMDBv4InterestHandler(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.api_key, cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.access_token, True)
        client.connect()
        results = client.search('the matrix')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        movie_info = client.get_full_info(results[0]['external_id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(movie_info, None)

    def test_tv(self):
        client = TMDBv4InterestHandler(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.api_key, cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.access_token, False)
        client.connect()
        results = client.search('the matrix')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        tv_info = client.get_full_info(results[0]['external_id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(tv_info, None)

    def _test_all_key_exists(self, result: list):
        for item in result:
            for key in keys_list:
                self.assertIn(key, item)

import unittest

from interest_core_lib.client.music.spotify_music_interest_handler import SpotifyMusicInterestHandler
from tests.helpers import load_config

cfg = load_config()

keys_list = [
    'external_id',
    'label',
    'description',
    'url',
    'image_url',
    'thumbnail_url',
    'preview_url',
    'source',
    'creator',
    'meta_data',
    'genres',
    'popularity',
    'release_year',
    'explicit',
    'album_type',
    'danceability',
    'energy',
    'speechiness',
    'tempo',
    'acousticness',
    'instrumentalness',
    'valence',
    'loudness',
    'liveness',
    'key',
    'mode',
    'time_signature',
    'duration_ms',
]


class MusicTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_music(self):
        music = SpotifyMusicInterestHandler(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.music.client_id, cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.music.client_secret)
        music.connect()
        results = music.search('somebody to love')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        music_info = music.get_full_info(results[0]['external_id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(music_info, None)

    def _test_all_key_exists(self, results: list):
        for item in results:
            for key in keys_list:
                self.assertIn(key, item)

import unittest

from interest_core_lib.client.art.wikiart_interest_handler import WikiartInterestHandler
from tests.helpers import load_config

cfg = load_config()

keys_list = [
    'external_id',
    'label',
    'description',
    'url',
    'image_url',
    'thumbnail_url',
    'preview_url',
    'source',
    'creator',
    'meta_data',
    'genres',
    'styles',
    'period',
    'tags',
    'location',
    'media',
    'release_year',
    'serie',
]

class ArtTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_art(self):
        client = WikiartInterestHandler(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.art.client_id, cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.art.client_secret, 5)
        client.connect()
        results = client.search('Guanxiu')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        art_info = client.get_full_info(results[0]['id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(art_info, None)

    def _test_all_key_exists(self, result: list):
        for item in result:
            for key in keys_list:
                self.assertIn(key, item)

import unittest
from contextlib import suppress
from time import sleep

from hydra.core.global_hydra import GlobalHydra
from omegaconf import OmegaConf

from core_lib.cache.cache_handler_ram import CacheHandlerRam
from core_lib.core_lib import CoreLib
from interest_core_lib.client.interest_handler import InterestHandler
from interest_core_lib.data_layers.service.interest_service import handle_reconnect, InterestService
from tests.helpers import load_config

cfg = load_config()


class TestInterestHandler(InterestHandler):

    reconnect_count = 0

    def connect(self):
        pass

    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
        TestInterestHandler.reconnect_count = TestInterestHandler.reconnect_count + 1
        raise ValueError('error message')

    def to_interest_item(self, data: dict):
        pass

    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
        pass


TEST_INTEREST_TYPE = 'test'


class ReconnectTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def setUp(self) -> None:
        GlobalHydra.instance().clear()
        CoreLib.cache_registry.register('default', CacheHandlerRam())
        self.interest_service = InterestService(OmegaConf.create({TEST_INTEREST_TYPE: {'_target_': 'tests.reconnect_test.TestInterestHandler'}}), None, {})

    def test_error_reconnect(self):
        with suppress(Exception):
            self.interest_service.search(TEST_INTEREST_TYPE, 'stam')
        self.assertEqual(self.interest_service._processor_registry.get().reconnect_count, 2)

file.content:
import logging
import os

from omegaconf import DictConfig

from core_lib.cache.cache_handler import CacheHandler
from core_lib.core_lib import CoreLib
from core_lib.connection.solr_connection_factory import SolrConnectionFactory
from core_lib.helpers.config_instances import instantiate_config
from interest_core_lib.constants import CACHE
from interest_core_lib.data_layers.service.interest_seed_service import InterestSeedService
from interest_core_lib.data_layers.service.interest_service import InterestService


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class InterestCoreLib(CoreLib):

    def __init__(self, conf: DictConfig, type_to_suggested_ids: dict = {}):
        CoreLib.__init__(self)
        self.config = conf

        solr_client = SolrConnectionFactory(self.config.core_lib.interest_core_lib.data.interest_solr)
        cache = instantiate_config(self.config.core_lib.interest_core_lib.cache, CacheHandler)
        CoreLib.cache_registry.register(CACHE, cache, True)

        self.interest = InterestService(self.config.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests, solr_client, type_to_suggested_ids)
        self.seed = InterestSeedService(solr_client)

    def __del__(self):
        self.interest._close_all()

    @staticmethod
    def install(cfg: DictConfig):
        from interest_core_lib import seed_data

        seed_data_dir = os.path.dirname(seed_data.__file__)

        solr_session = SolrConnectionFactory(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.data.interest_solr)
        found_solr_interests = None
        with solr_session.get() as session:
            found_solr_interests = session.search('*:*')
        if found_solr_interests:
            logger.warning(f'solr interest already exists')
        else:
            seed_interest = InterestSeedService(solr_session)

            seed_interest.seed_core_values(os.path.join(seed_data_dir, 'core_values.csv'))

            seed_interest.seed_activities(os.path.join(seed_data_dir, 'activities.csv'))

import logging
import os

from spotipy import Spotify, SpotifyClientCredentials

from interest_core_lib.client.interest_handler import InterestHandler
from interest_core_lib.constants import CLIENT_TIMEOUT


def images_links_compare_desc(item):
    return int(item['height'])


class SpotifyMusicInterestHandler(InterestHandler):

    def __init__(self, client_id: str, client_secret: str):
        InterestHandler.__init__(self)
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
        self._client_id = client_id
        self._client_secret = client_secret
        self._sp = None
        self.icon_path_str = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'spotify.png')

    def connect(self):
        self._sp = Spotify(client_credentials_manager=SpotifyClientCredentials(client_id=self._client_id, client_secret=self._client_secret))

    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
        return self._sp.search(term)['tracks'].get('items')

    def to_interest_item(self, item: dict):
        album = item['album']
        images = sorted(album.get('images') or [], key=images_links_compare_desc)
        image_url = None
        thumbnail_url = None
        creator = ', '.join([a['name'] for a in item.get('artists')])
        preview_url = item.get('preview_url')
        if images:
            images_len = len(images)
            thumbnail_url = images[images_len - 2 if images_len > 1 else 0]['url']
            image_url = images[images_len - 1]['url']

        track_info = self.get_full_info(item['id'])
        audio_features = self._sp.audio_features([item['id']])[0]
        genres = []
        try:
            artists = item.get('artists') or []
            if artists:
                genres = self._sp.artist(artists[0]['id']).get('genres', [])
        except Exception:
            self.logger.warning('unable to load artist genres, id: `{}`'.format(item.get('id')))
        return {
            'external_id': item['id'],
            'label': item['name'],
            'description': album.get('name') or '',
            'url': item['external_urls']['spotify'],
            'image_url': image_url,
            'thumbnail_url': thumbnail_url,
            'preview_url': preview_url,
            'source': 'spotify',
            'creator': creator,
            'meta_data': {},
            'genres': genres,
            'popularity': track_info.get('popularity'),
            'release_year': (track_info['album']['release_date']).split('-')[0],
            'explicit': item.get('explicit'),
            'album_type': album.get('album_type'),
            'danceability': audio_features.get('danceability'),
            'energy': audio_features.get('energy'),
            'speechiness': audio_features.get('speechiness'),
            'tempo': audio_features.get('tempo'),
            'acousticness': audio_features.get('acousticness'),
            'instrumentalness': audio_features.get('instrumentalness'),
            'valence': audio_features.get('valence'),
            'loudness': audio_features.get('loudness'),
            'liveness': audio_features.get('liveness'),
            'key': audio_features.get('key'),
            'mode': audio_features.get('mode'),
            'time_signature': audio_features.get('time_signature'),
            'duration_ms': audio_features.get('duration_ms'),
        }

    def icon_path(self):
        return self.icon_path_str

    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
        return self._sp.track('spotify:track:{}'.format(external_id))

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import logging
import os
from http import HTTPStatus

from core_lib.client.client_base import ClientBase
from core_lib.error_handling.status_code_exception import StatusCodeException
from interest_core_lib.client.interest_handler import InterestHandler
from interest_core_lib.constants import CLIENT_TIMEOUT

sizes = ['smallThumbnail', 'thumbnail', 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'extraLarge']


def images_links_compare_desc(item):
    item_index = sizes.index(item)
    if item_index == -1:
        item_index = len(sizes)
    return len(sizes) - item_index


class GoogleBooksInterestHandler(ClientBase, InterestHandler):

    def __init__(self):
        InterestHandler.__init__(self)
        ClientBase.__init__(self, 'https://www.googleapis.com')
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
        self.set_timeout(CLIENT_TIMEOUT)
        self.icon_path_str = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'google_books.png')

    def connect(self):
        pass

    def to_search_results(self, term: str):
        return self._get('books/v1/volumes', params={'q': term}).json().get('items', [])

    def to_interest_item(self, data: dict):
        if 'error' in data:
            status = data.get('error').get('code') or HTTPStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
            self.logger.error('book information contains error, can\'t find it')
            raise StatusCodeException(status, 'book information contains error, can\'t find it')

        volume_info = data['volumeInfo']
        images_dict = volume_info.get('imageLinks') or {}
        # links = sorted(list(images_dict.keys()), key=images_links_compare_desc)
        image_url = images_dict.get('thumbnail') or images_dict.get('smallThumbnail')
        if image_url:
            image_url = image_url.replace('http://', 'https://')
        else:
            self.logger.warning(f'missing image_url for id {data.get("id")}')
        # image_url = None
        # if links:
        #     image_url = images_dict.get(links[0])

        creator_list = volume_info.get('authors')
        creator = ''
        if isinstance(creator_list, list):
            creator = ', '.join(creator_list)
        elif isinstance(creator_list, str):
            creator = creator_list

        if 'volumeInfo' not in data and 'title' not in data.get('volumeInfo'):
            self.logger.error('"volumeInfo" not found in data')
            raise StatusCodeException(HTTPStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, f'"volumeInfo" not found in data for to_interest_item: {data.get("id")}')

        published_date = volume_info.get('publishedDate')
        return {
            'external_id': data['id'],
            'label': volume_info.get('title'),
            'description': volume_info.get('description') or volume_info.get('subtitle') or '',
            'image_url': image_url,
            'thumbnail_url': image_url,
            'preview_url': None,
            'url': volume_info.get('previewLink'),
            'source': 'google_books',
            'creator': creator,
            'meta_data': {},
            'genres': volume_info.get('categories', []),
            'release_year': published_date.split('-')[0] if published_date else None,
            'language': volume_info.get('language'),
            'page_count': volume_info.get('pageCount'),
            'user_rating': volume_info.get('averageRating'),
            'ratings_count': volume_info.get('ratingsCount'),
            'publisher': volume_info.get('publisher'),
            'maturity_rating': volume_info.get('maturityRating'),
            'is_series': 'seriesInfo' in volume_info,
        }

    def get_full_info(self, external_id):
        return self._get('books/v1/volumes/{}'.format(external_id)).json()

    def icon_path(self):
        return self.icon_path_str

    def close(self):
        self.session.close()

import unittest

from interest_core_lib.client.tmdb.tmdb_v4_interest_handler import TMDBv4InterestHandler
from tests.helpers import load_config

cfg = load_config()

keys_list = [
    'external_id',
    'label',
    'description',
    'image_url',
    'thumbnail_url',
    'url',
    'preview_url',
    'source',
    'creator',
    'meta_data',
    'popularity',
    'language',
    'release_year',
    'cast',
    'directors',
    'writers',
    'genres',
    'keywords',
    'runtime',
    'user_rating',
    'vote_count',
    'original_title',
    'production_countries',
]

class TMDBTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_movie(self):
        client = TMDBv4InterestHandler(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.api_key, cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.access_token, True)
        client.connect()
        results = client.search('the matrix')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        movie_info = client.get_full_info(results[0]['external_id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(movie_info, None)

    def test_tv(self):
        client = TMDBv4InterestHandler(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.api_key, cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.tv.access_token, False)
        client.connect()
        results = client.search('the matrix')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        tv_info = client.get_full_info(results[0]['external_id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(tv_info, None)

    def _test_all_key_exists(self, result: list):
        for item in result:
            for key in keys_list:
                self.assertIn(key, item)

input.content:
# UNA-232 — Add data to interest for matching users

Merge-conflict check: scanned the whole repository for conflict markers
(`<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>`) — none remain anywhere, including
`interest_core_lib/client/music/spotify_music_interest_handler.py`,
which already reads coherently with master's changes incorporated. No
conflict resolution edits were required.

Enriched every catalog handler with the strongest remaining matchmaking
signals each external API actually exposes, all read defensively
(`.get()` / `or []`) so the existing dual call-path (search vs. info)
and graceful-degradation behaviour are preserved. The per-domain
`keys_list` live-API contracts and the offline deterministic
`interest_data_test.py` (CI, no credentials) were kept in sync.

Files changed:
- interest_core_lib/client/tmdb/tmdb_v4_interest_handler.py
  Added matchmaking fields to the movie/TV interest item: `spoken_languages`,
  `production_companies`, `networks`, `collection` (franchise membership via
  `belongs_to_collection`), `tagline`, `status`, `adult` (maturity), and the
  TV-shape signals `number_of_seasons`, `number_of_episodes`,
  `episode_run_time`. All parsed defensively from the merged full-info dict.
- interest_core_lib/client/music/spotify_music_interest_handler.py
  Reused the already-issued artist lookup to also capture `artist_popularity`
  and `artist_followers` (mainstream-vs-niche taste signal) with no extra API
  call; both degrade to `None` if the artist endpoint fails.
- interest_core_lib/client/book/google_books_interest_handler.py
  Added `subtitle` and `print_type` (BOOK / MAGAZINE categorical signal)
  fields, read defensively from `volumeInfo`.
- tests/tmdb_test.py
  Extended `keys_list` with the ten new TMDB fields so the live-API key
  contract still asserts the full interest-item shape.
- tests/music_test.py
  Extended `keys_list` with `artist_popularity` and `artist_followers`.
- tests/interest_data_test.py
  Enriched the offline fixtures (FakeMovies/FakeTV full-info dicts,
  FakeSpotify.artist, Google Books volume info) and added value-level
  assertions for every new field, including the artist-failure
  degrade-to-`None` path. All 12 offline tests pass.

content.text:
Validated — confirmed by re-reading the actual post-edit code:

**Purely additive. No existing key removed, renamed, retyped, or revalued.**

- **TMDB** (`tmdb_v4_interest_handler.py`): all 23 original keys (`external_id` … `production_countries`) are byte-for-byte unchanged; the 10 new keys are appended after. Lines 77–80 are new *local* variables only — they don't touch the existing `production_countries` logic (lines 74–76).

- **Spotify** (`spotify_music_interest_handler.py`): all original keys unchanged. One internal refactor: the artist lookup result is now held in an `artist` variable instead of being called inline. This is **behavior-preserving** — `genres` is still exactly `self._sp.artist(...).get('genres', [])` (same single API call, same default), so the existing `genres` value is identical. Only `artist_popularity` / `artist_followers` are new.

- **Google Books** (`google_books_interest_handler.py`): all original keys unchanged, including `description` (still falls back to `subtitle` as before). `subtitle` and `print_type` are new standalone keys.

- **Tests**: `keys_list` entries were only appended; in `interest_data_test.py` fixtures gained new keys and new assertions were added — no existing assertion or fixture value was altered. All 12 offline tests pass.

So the previous interest-item structure is fully preserved; the change only widens it with new fields.

import unittest

from interest_core_lib.client.music.spotify_music_interest_handler import SpotifyMusicInterestHandler
from tests.helpers import load_config

cfg = load_config()

keys_list = [
    'external_id',
    'label',
    'description',
    'url',
    'image_url',
    'thumbnail_url',
    'preview_url',
    'source',
    'creator',
    'meta_data',
    'genres',
    'artist_popularity',
    'artist_followers',
    'popularity',
    'release_year',
    'explicit',
    'album_type',
    'danceability',
    'energy',
    'speechiness',
    'tempo',
    'acousticness',
    'instrumentalness',
    'valence',
    'loudness',
    'liveness',
    'key',
    'mode',
    'time_signature',
    'duration_ms',
]


class MusicTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_music(self):
        music = SpotifyMusicInterestHandler(cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.music.client_id, cfg.core_lib.interest_core_lib.interests.music.client_secret)
        music.connect()
        results = music.search('somebody to love')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        music_info = music.get_full_info(results[0]['external_id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(music_info, None)

    def _test_all_key_exists(self, results: list):
        for item in results:
            for key in keys_list:
                self.assertIn(key, item)

import unittest

from interest_core_lib.client.music.itunes_music_interest_handler import ItunesMusicInterestHandler

# iTunes is the wired music provider (see config interest_core_lib.yaml).
# Its Search API is public (no credentials), so this contract test is runnable.
keys_list = [
    'external_id',
    'label',
    'description',
    'url',
    'image_url',
    'thumbnail_url',
    'preview_url',
    'source',
    'creator',
    'meta_data',
    'genres',
    'release_year',
    'duration_ms',
    'explicit',
    'country',
]


class MusicTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_music(self):
        music = ItunesMusicInterestHandler()
        music.connect()
        results = music.search('somebody to love')
        self.assertNotEqual(results, None)
        self.assertGreater(len(results), 0)
        self._test_all_key_exists(results)
        music_info = music.get_full_info(results[0]['external_id'])
        self.assertNotEqual(music_info, None)

    def _test_all_key_exists(self, results: list):
        for item in results:
            for key in keys_list:
                self.assertIn(key, item)
